subject: Sports
title: Sleep: The Ultimate Recovery Tool
description: A 60-second lesson on Sleep: The Ultimate Recovery Tool.

hook.text: Roger Federer sleeps 12 hours a day. LeBron James sleeps 10. The best recovery tool is free.

buildup.text: During deep sleep, the body releases human growth hormone—essential for muscle repair and recovery.

discovery.text: Stanford basketball players who extended sleep to 10 hours improved sprint times and free-throw accuracy by 9%.

twist.text: Chronic sleep deprivation increases injury risk by 60%. It impairs reaction time worse than moderate alcohol.

climax.text: Elite teams now hire sleep coaches and design travel schedules around circadian rhythms.

punchline.text: The cheapest performance enhancer in sport is eight hours of sleep.

quiz.question: How much does chronic sleep deprivation increase injury risk?
